_source: Primary literature biophysical priors for A. flavus uricase (Q00511) thermal/pH stability behavior.
_protein: Aspergillus flavus uricase (urate oxidase, rasburicase parent), UniProt Q00511

thermal_kinetics.wt_Tm_source: Imani & Shahmohamadnejad 2017, 3 Biotech 7:201, DOI:10.1007/s13205-017-0841-3, PMID:28667645. Lactose-supplemented condition raises Tm to 37°C; this is the WT-only value.
thermal_kinetics.wt_thermal_halflife_source: Imani & Shahmohamadnejad 2017 (PMID:28667645) — 'UOX half-life is almost 38 min' at 40°C in buffer.
thermal_kinetics.wt_thermal_halflife_independent_source: Rezaeian Marjani et al. 2020, Iran J Biotechnol 18:e2662, DOI:10.30498/IJB.2020.2662, PMID:33850949 — 'Estimation of half life for wild-type enzyme demonstrated 38.5 min' at thermal-inactivation temperature (paper uses temperatures spanning enzyme's thermal range; cross-check confirms order-of-magnitude).
thermal_kinetics.wt_thermal_inactivation_rate_constant_so: Imani & Shahmohamadnejad 2017 (PMID:28667645) — first-order inactivation rate k = 0.018 min^-1 at 40°C.
thermal_kinetics.activation_energy_source_note: Typical activation energy for cooperative protein unfolding of a 35-kDa globular enzyme; range 150-300 kJ/mol in the literature (Daniel & Danson 2010, Trends Biochem Sci 35:584). Not directly measured for A. flavus uricase; legacy parameter retained for reference. The two-regime model in analyze.py uses ΔH_vH (below) for the cooperative-unfolding component and a much smaller residual Ea = 50 kJ/mol for the irreversible-loss chemistry, so the 200 kJ/mol value here is not actively used in the composite retention prediction.
thermal_kinetics.van_t_hoff_enthalpy_source_note: Van't Hoff unfolding enthalpy for a 35-kDa globular protein. Typical range 300-600 kJ/mol (Privalov 1979, Adv Protein Chem 33:167; Robertson & Murphy 1997, Chem Rev 97:1251). 400 kJ/mol is a conservative midpoint. This sets the steepness of the two-state unfolding transition centered at Tm = 27°C. Higher ΔH = sharper transition, lower fraction unfolded at temperatures even slightly below Tm. Uncertainty bracket in analyze.py varies this across 300-500 kJ/mol.

pH_stability.wt_pH_optimum_source: Rezaeian Marjani et al. 2020 (PMID:33850949) — pH optimum 7 for WT, broadened to 7-10 in disulfide mutants. Other literature consistently places A. flavus uricase pH optimum at 8-9. Use 8.5 as midpoint.

ph_residual_activity_curve._units: fraction of pH-optimum activity (0-1), measured for soluble enzyme
ph_residual_activity_curve._caveat: Activity != stability. These are activity readouts; stability (tetramer integrity, irreversible unfolding) is generally retained over a wider pH range than activity. For shio-koji we care about *retained activity after 7-14 days at pH X*, which combines both axes.

pH_stability.tetramer_dissociation_source_note: Most homotetrameric enzymes with key interface salt bridges dissociate below pH ~4 as Asp/Glu protonate; A. flavus uricase has been crystallized down to pH 5 (Retailleau et al. 2004, Acta Cryst D60:453, PDB depositions span pH 5-8) without tetramer loss, suggesting threshold below 5. Below pH 4 is a soft estimate; not directly measured for Q00511.

salt_effects_on_stability._summary: NaCl at moderate-to-high concentration is a kosmotrope on the Hofmeister series (mild salting-out behavior at 15-20%). The net effect on globular protein stability is *stabilizing* via preferential hydration of the native state — opposite direction from the protease-activity effect modelled in comp-001.
salt_effects_on_stability.preferential_hydration_source: Timasheff 2002, Biochemistry 41:13473 — typical kosmotrope-mediated stabilization factor for globular proteins at ~3 M NaCl (~17.5%) is +5 to +20% increase in apparent stability (ΔΔG_unfolding ~ +0.5 to +2 kJ/mol). Conservative midpoint: +15%.
salt_effects_on_stability.interface_ion_pair_disruption_source: Counterbalancing effect: at very high [NaCl] (>2 M / >12%), Na+ and Cl- ions partially screen interfacial salt bridges, reducing their stabilization energy by ~5-15% per Bjerrum-length estimate. Net effect on tetramer interfaces is small but non-zero subtraction from the preferential-hydration gain.
salt_effects_on_stability._net_factor_calculation: 1.15 (preferential hydration) * 0.92 (ion pair screening) = 1.058 — small net stabilization.

shio_koji_reference_conditions._source: Standard home-fermentation shio-koji protocol (engineered-koji-protocol.md §15, koji-home-fermentation.md). Salt set above the 12-13% Aspergillus oryzae growth limit to halt living-cell metabolism after koji harvest.
